description:
You are the site administrator of a small three-node cluster.  
    Each node keeps its local user list in:

        /home/user/cluster/nodes/node1/users.txt
        /home/user/cluster/nodes/node2/users.txt
        /home/user/cluster/nodes/node3/users.txt

    The files already exist and contain simple, **one-username-per-line**
    records.  Unfortunately the lists have drifted out of sync, and you have
    to:

        1. Collect every username that appears on **any** node.
        2. Work out which usernames are missing from every individual node.
        3. Produce a human-readable audit log in a very strict format.

    What you must deliver
    ---------------------

    1. Directory  
          /home/user/cluster_audit  
       (create it if it does not exist).

    2. File  
          /home/user/cluster_audit/all_users.txt  
       – The consolidated, **alphabetically sorted** list of every distinct
       username found across all three nodes.  
       – Format: one username per line, **UNIX line-endings**, no blank lines
       before, after or between entries.

    3. File  
          /home/user/cluster_audit/audit.log  
       – This is the report the automated grader will read.  
       – The content must follow *exactly* the template shown below:

            AUDIT REPORT - YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ

            NODE: node1
            MISSING_USERS(2): eve,frank

            NODE: node2
            MISSING_USERS(2): bob,dave

            NODE: node3
            MISSING_USERS(2): dave,eve

            SUMMARY
            TOTAL_UNIQUE_USERS: 6
            NODES_SCANNED: 3

         Rules and clarifications:

         • The timestamp is in UTC ISO-8601 format (e.g.
           “2025-01-30T14:07:09Z”).  
         • After the first line there is **exactly one blank line**.  
         • Each “NODE:” block is separated by **one blank line**.  
         • `MISSING_USERS(<number>):` shows the count of usernames absent
           from that node followed by a colon and a **comma-separated
           list with no spaces** (or the literal “NONE” if the node is 100 %
           in sync).  
         • “SUMMARY” appears after one blank line following the last node
           block, and is followed by the two key/value lines shown.  
         • Do not add any extra lines at the end of the file.

    4. The command session that creates these artefacts may print anything
       you find useful, but the two files above must be present **in the exact
       locations and formats described** by the time your terminal session
       ends.

    Verification
    ------------

    The automated test will:

        • cat /home/user/cluster_audit/all_users.txt and compare it byte-for-byte
          with the expected list.  
        • grep mandatory lines inside /home/user/cluster_audit/audit.log:  
              “NODE: node1”, “MISSING_USERS(2): eve,frank”  
              “NODE: node2”, “MISSING_USERS(2): bob,dave”  
              “NODE: node3”, “MISSING_USERS(2): dave,eve”  
              “SUMMARY”, “TOTAL_UNIQUE_USERS: 6”, “NODES_SCANNED: 3”  
        • Check that the report’s first line matches the pattern  
              ^AUDIT REPORT - [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$  
        • Confirm there are no extraneous blank lines anywhere else.

    Your goal: generate the two files exactly as specified.
truth:
Initial directory tree & contents before the agent starts:

    /home/user/cluster/nodes/node1/users.txt
        alice
        bob
        charlie
        dave

    /home/user/cluster/nodes/node2/users.txt
        alice
        charlie
        eve
        frank

    /home/user/cluster/nodes/node3/users.txt
        alice
        bob
        charlie
        frank

    Expected final artefacts created by the agent:

    1. /home/user/cluster_audit/all_users.txt  
       Contents (including final newline, UNIX line endings):

        alice
        bob
        charlie
        dave
        eve
        frank

    2. /home/user/cluster_audit/audit.log  
       Contents must match the template; the only dynamic part that the test
       ignores is the timestamp on the very first line.  For reference, with a
       dummy timestamp the full correct file would look like:

            AUDIT REPORT - 2025-01-30T14:07:09Z

            NODE: node1
            MISSING_USERS(2): eve,frank

            NODE: node2
            MISSING_USERS(2): bob,dave

            NODE: node3
            MISSING_USERS(2): dave,eve

            SUMMARY
            TOTAL_UNIQUE_USERS: 6
            NODES_SCANNED: 3

       The grader will assert:
         • the six fixed informational lines are present verbatim;
         • exactly one blank line appears in every required location;
         • no extra text or trailing blanks are present.

    The agent does not need elevated privileges; the user account running the
    session owns /home/user and everything beneath it.
name: task_000000_06f30c25
